_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-14 SPRN Patient Care During Transport (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). MCA Optional — SPRN program participation required.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-14: SPRN Patient Care During Transport — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Define patient care procedures, physical needs assessment, and crew safety protocols for EMS personnel providing care to SPRN patients during transport.

sections.title: Pre-Transport Coordination with Facilities
content: Coordinate with SENDING FACILITY before patient pickup:
— Confirm patient clinical status, current vital signs, and recent medications
— Confirm life support status decision (resuscitation status) — this must be established BEFORE transport
— Confirm IV access and current infusions (if any)
— Obtain current medication list and any pending treatments
Coordinate with RECEIVING FACILITY before transport:
— Confirm capability to receive SPRN patient in appropriate isolation
— Provide patient classification (Protocol 10-11) and expected ETA
— Discuss any anticipated interventions en route so receiving team is prepared

sections.title: Physical Needs Assessment
content: Minimize physical contact and invasive procedures during SPRN transport — each touch = contamination risk
Vital signs: use NON-INVASIVE methods preferentially
— SpO2: use disposable or dedicated SPRN pulse oximetry probe
— BP: non-invasive cuff — use dedicated SPRN equipment (decontaminate or dispose after)
— Bluetooth/wireless vital sign monitoring preferred if available — reduces direct contact
Temperature: tympanic or non-contact thermometer — dispose or decontaminate after use
Cardiac monitoring: attach leads before transport; do NOT reach into MITD for lead adjustment during transport if possible
IV access: maintain existing access; do NOT start new IV/IO inside MITD if avoidable — pass IV lines through MITD access ports
Avoid sharps: sharps injury in SPRN transport is a critical safety event — use needleless systems; never recap needles; dispose in sharps container immediately

sections.title: Sharps Safety
content: AVOID sharps during SPRN transport whenever possible
If sharps use is unavoidable:
— Use needleless IV systems and prefilled syringes
— Immediate disposal in sharps container — never recap
— Two-provider acknowledgment before any sharps use in patient compartment
Sharps injury during SPRN transport:
— Immediately notify partner and SPRN coordinator
— Flush wound with water if available
— Continue transport to receiving facility — do NOT stop mid-transport
— At receiving facility: report injury; receive immediate medical evaluation
— Follow agency and MDHHS HCID post-exposure protocol

sections.title: Life Support Status
content: Life support status (full resuscitation vs. comfort care vs. DNR) MUST be confirmed before SPRN transport begins
Do NOT initiate resuscitation in an SPRN transport without prior confirmation of resuscitation status
Cardiac arrest during SPRN transport: Protocol 10-18 applies — STOP transport; no interventions; contact CHECC
Advanced airway management in SPRN transport: extremely high-risk for PPE breach and crew exposure — contact online MC; use only if life-threatening and patient has resuscitation status

sections.title: Crew Safety — PPE Breach Protocol
content: PPE BREACH: any event where potentially infectious material contacts provider skin or mucous membrane through a defect in PPE
Examples: glove tear, PAPR hood failure, splash to face, needlestick
IF PPE BREACH OCCURS:
1. Alert partner immediately — stop all patient care tasks
2. If possible, partner provides replacement PPE layer
3. Minor breach (glove only): replace outer glove layer; continue transport
4. Major breach (PAPR failure, face splash): exit patient compartment if possible; notify SPRN coordinator; contact online MC
5. Continue transport to receiving facility — do NOT abandon patient
6. Document breach type, time, and exposure location
7. At receiving facility: report breach to safety officer; receive immediate medical evaluation

sections.title: Emergency Doffing Area
content: An emergency doffing area must be identified and pre-planned before each SPRN transport
Emergency doffing area: a safe, contained location where a provider can safely remove contaminated PPE in an emergency
Identify location during pre-transport briefing — may be at sending facility, along route, or at Patient Transfer Point
Emergency doffing supplies: additional PPE, biohazard bags, disinfectant wipes — carried in chase vehicle (Protocol 10-12)

mca_selections_required.label: SPRN Program Participation — MCA authorization for SPRN protocols 10-10 through 10-18
mca_selections_required.description: MCA Optional: All SPRN protocols apply only to SPRN-designated agencies.

deterioration_flags: Sharps injury — notify partner and SPRN coordinator immediately; continue transport; do NOT stop mid-transport for injury management; receive evaluation at destination
Major PPE breach — exit patient area if possible; notify SPRN coordinator; continue transport; receive evaluation at destination
Cardiac arrest — STOP; Protocol 10-18 applies; contact CHECC
scope_notes: MCA Optional — SPRN only. Minimize interventions and sharps use during SPRN transport. Life support status must be confirmed BEFORE transport begins — resuscitation during SPRN transport requires Protocol 10-18. PPE breach is treated as a safety emergency — alert partner, document, and receive evaluation at destination. Continue transport — do NOT abandon patient mid-transport.
